div.p: Where gossip protocols sit on the consistency-availability spectrum
Key Insight: Gossip trades immediate consistency for availability and partition tolerance. Updates spread in O(log N) rounds, guaranteeing eventual convergence even under failures.

g.text: Availability | Always accept writes | Majority required
Partition Response | Both sides accept writes | Minority rejects writes
Convergence | O(log N) rounds | 1 RTT (when available)
Fault Tolerance | Any number of failures | Minority failures only
Scalability | O(1) per-node bandwidth | O(N) leader bandwidth
